entries.description: Operator confirmed timing (good) and Set 018 budget ($5.00) at session start; budget written to budget.json. Per memory feedback_budget_question_scope, asked once at set start. Cost projection in spec was $0 metered; $5 ceiling allowed one ad-hoc consultation if audit surfaced an unexpected pattern.

Registered Session 1 start via ai_router.session_state.register_session_start with orchestrator=claude-code/anthropic/claude-opus-4-7/normal (effort matches spec config). Snapshot flipped to in-progress; work_started event appended idempotently to session-events.jsonl.

Audited dabbler-homehealthcare-accessdb per spec Step 3. Findings diverged from spec premise: (a) NOT A GIT REPOSITORY at all (no .git, no parent .git); (b) no Python venv, no ai_router/, no dabbler-ai-router pip install (extraction runs from scripts/run_extraction.ps1 driving Access COM via PowerShell); (c) no session-set scaffolding - sessions tracked as 21-row ledger inside CLAUDE.md with closing BATONs at docs/sessions/NNN-<slug>.md (sessions 001-005 complete, 006-021 planned); (d) custom UAT pattern using standalone HTML UAT Checklist Editor at github.com/darndestdabbler/uat-checklist-editor with per-set checklist JSON at docs/uat-checklists/, distinct from the dabbler-ai-orchestration extension's UAT Checklist tree; (e) extension state already aligned (only darndestdabbler.dabbler-ai-orchestration installed; no legacy dabbler-session-sets). Most of spec's acceptance criteria N/A by audit.

Probed extension's session-set discovery requirements at tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/fileSystem.ts. discoverRoots() iterates vscode.workspace.workspaceFolders + their git-worktrees and looks for docs/session-sets/ relative to each; readSessionSets() hard-requires only spec.md per set, every other artifact (activity-log.json, session-state.json, change-log.md, CANCELLED.md, ai-assignment.md, <set>-uat-checklist.json) is optional and degrades gracefully. No co-located ai_router/ requirement; no router-config.yaml check; no Python introspection. Conclusion: Explorer-only adoption tier is structurally supported today - the gap is documentation + bootstrap-flow guidance, not extension code.

Surfaced audit findings + four candidate session-set organizations for healthcare-accessdb (bootstrap-only minimal; phase-themed retrofit; historical+rolling+future-placeholder; feature-coverage axes). Recommended Candidate B (phase-themed retrofit, ~7-9 sets, each 1-3 sessions). Operator's first redirect: recognize lightweight-adoption as a real abstraction the framework should support directly - some projects want session-set organization without the router. Operator's second redirect: AI should infer workflow patterns from the repo and propose multiple session-set organizations to the human, but ask the question through the extension's getting-started prompt - not from this orchestration repo. Operator's third redirect: identify abstract patterns the AI can use (input artifacts / output artifacts / cross-cutting themes / stated objectives / inferred organizational patterns / etc.) but leave the actual application to the downstream orchestrator. Net redirect: scope shifts from 'migrate healthcare-accessdb' to 'extend adoption-bootstrap with Lightweight tier + organization-design dialog with abstract pattern catalog.' healthcare-accessdb is NOT touched by Set 018.

Authored ai-assignment.md with reshaped scope - Track A only (extension's getting-started prompt + canonical doc); Track B (healthcare-accessdb work) explicitly out of scope and owned by downstream orchestrator. Six-section shape adapted from Set 015's per-repo plan template: Findings (lightweight-tier abstraction need + supporting evidence); Current state (existing prompt + canonical doc + workflow doc + README); Drift items vs operator redirect; Edit plan (4a Step 4.5 insertion; 4b+4c Step 6 rewrite with abstract pattern catalog; 4d Steps 7+9 branched; 4e workflow-doc disambiguator paragraph; 4f spec reshape pointer; 4g memory updates + close-out); Risk callouts (doc bloat, vocabulary collision, fresh-chat-AI skip risk, mechanical-walk failure mode); Out of scope. Revised acceptance criteria authored. Plan presented for explicit operator approval.

Operator approved the reshape plan and raised the set budget to $10 mid-session: 'Approve. And the budget can be $10 for this. Proceed. No more questions for me. Let's get this done.' Updated budget.json with raisedAt=2026-05-07. Proceeded with execution end-to-end without further questions per operator instruction.

Executed 4a. Edited Step 4 to acknowledge adoption tier as a parallel decision and frame cross-provider verification as opt-in via tier choice rather than the default. Inserted new Step 4.5 (Adoption tier) between Step 4 and Step 5: explicit Lightweight (L) vs Full (F) tier-choice dialog with definitions, when-each-fits guidance, branching instruction (L skips Step 5; F continues), coexistence sub-dialog (replace / parallel / index) for projects with their own session protocol, confirm-and-note close. Step 5 prologue note added: 'Skip this step entirely if Lightweight (L) was picked at Step 4.5.' No renumbering of existing Steps 5-9 - preserves all inbound links.

Executed 4b+4c. Replaced Step 6 (Plan alignment) body with new three-subsection structure: 'How to derive a decomposition' (instruction to propose 2-4 cross-cut candidates not slight variants); 'Abstract patterns the AI should consider' (7-pattern catalog as a 4-column table - input artifacts / output artifacts / cross-cutting themes / stated objectives / inferred organizational patterns / risk-or-dependency layers / stakeholder review boundaries; explicit don't-enumerate-all-every-time guidance); 'Propose 2-4 candidates, ask the human to pick' (proposal framing, what to include for each candidate, watch-out failure modes - over-decomposing, forced cuts, slight variants of one cut); greenfield projects sub-section; existing-projects-with-own-protocol sub-section pointing back to Step 4.5's coexistence dialog. Lightweight-tier reminder at top of Step 6 about checklist scope.

Executed 4d. Step 7 prologue added: Lightweight checklists are a strict subset (no budget.yaml, no router-config.yaml, no ai_router/ scaffolding); existing example checklist is full-tier and must be adapted-down for lightweight, not padded with router items the human opted out of. Step 9 prologue added: Lightweight projects skip Budget monitoring + General cost monitoring sections. Step 9 closing extended with new Lightweight closing note (replaces the zero-budget reminder for L tier): no budget no metrics no automated verification by tier choice not exception, Session Set Explorer is the dashboard, 'Start the next session' still works against existing protocol per Step 4.5 coexistence choice, can upgrade to F by re-running bootstrap.

Executed 4e. Added ~10-line blockquote callout at top of docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md's 'Cost-budgeted verification modes' section disambiguating *budget* tier (within-Full-adoption concept governing how verification calls are paid for) from *adoption* tier (Lightweight vs Full, chosen at bootstrap Step 4.5). Explicit clause: 'Lightweight-tier projects opt out of cross-provider verification and the ai_router/ machinery entirely as part of choosing that tier - not as a cost-budgeted exception. Everything below applies only to Full-adoption projects.' First sentence of the section's body changed from 'Every project that adopts this workflow' to 'Every Full-adoption project'.

Executed 4f. Inserted '⚠ Scope reshape (2026-05-07)' blockquote callout at top of Set 018's spec.md explaining the audit findings, the operator redirect, and the actual deliverable plan being ai-assignment.md. Original spec body preserved as historical context. Original spec acceptance criteria recorded as explicitly N/A-by-reshape.

Executed 4g (memory portion). Updated project_consumer_repos.md: rewrote healthcare-accessdb section with full audit findings (no git, no Python venv, no ai_router/, no session-set scaffolding, custom BATON protocol, standalone HTML UAT editor, Access COM extraction discipline) and explicit Lightweight-tier-candidate note pointing at the new bootstrap Step 4.5; trimmed drift-status section to reflect Sets 015/016/017 closure. Wrote new project_lightweight_tier_added_to_bootstrap.md capturing the Step 4.5 / Step 6 changes, vocabulary watch (budget tier vs adoption tier - both use the word 'tier'; always qualify), and the no-version-bump-needed observation (prompt at master so doc edit auto-picked-up). Updated MEMORY.md index: extended consumer-repos entry to mention Lightweight-tier-candidate; appended new lightweight-tier line.

Validation pass. adoption-bootstrap.md structure verified: Step 1, 2, 3, 4, 4.5 (NEW), 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 in order; 461 lines (was 386, +75 close to estimate of ~80). Step 4.5 inspection confirmed L/F dialog + coexistence sub-dialog rendered correctly; Step 6 inspection confirmed 7-pattern catalog table + watch-out failure modes rendered correctly; Step 5 prologue + Step 7 prologue + Step 9 prologue + Step 9 closing-note all present. ai-led-session-workflow.md disambiguator callout present at correct position (immediately after section heading). Set 018 spec reshape callout present at top. All internal markdown links use existing relative paths confirmed by grep against existing file targets. No 4xx-bound URLs introduced; no orphaned anchors.

Authored close-out artifacts. change-log.md captures audit findings, scope reshape (3 stacked operator redirects), what landed (doc edits + memory updates), what did NOT change (no healthcare-accessdb edits, no extension TS, no version bump, no README edit), cost reconciliation ($0 metered actual vs $0 projected; $10 budget unspent), risks acknowledged at execution with status, queued follow-ups (none blocking; README mention + worked Lightweight example as future candidates). disposition.json: status=completed, verification_method=api, verification_message_ids=[] (operator review of ai-assignment.md before execution + read-back validation served as verification surface; no verifier route invoked per spec cost projection $0 metered + memory feedback_ai_router_usage). activity-log.json: this file.
